summary: PR #2294 repaired many near-matches where text was close but reloc/data values were wrong, including wrong called functions, wrong float literals or float data references, wrong collision helper calls, and signature/header mismatches. The author explicitly tied the issue to functions that had only wrong `bl` instructions and suspected workflows that did not start from m2c or did not diff with `functionRelocDiffs=data_value`. The bot reported 6 new matches and 2 unmatched-item improvements, but the author noted that the report appeared to undercount fixes involving data values.

what_changed: Corrected near-matches across 24 files, mostly by replacing wrong call targets, wrong float literals, or wrong data-symbol references.
Repaired several Kirby copied-power collision transitions by changing helper calls such as ft_80082708/ft_800827A0/ft_80081D0C and ftCommon_8007D5D4/ftCommon_8007D7FC in context-specific ways.
Updated the ftAnim_80070458 local declaration/call in ftKb_Init.c from a proto wrapper with 3 arguments to the real function signature using a tobj index and frame argument.
Changed ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 declaration from one Fighter_GObj parameter to victim and gobj parameters, matching new call sites in implemented Yoshi-capture animation functions.
Implemented ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_1_Anim and ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_0_Anim, which the bot reported as two new 100% matches.
Removed at least one explicit fake-match construct in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c: dummy locals and a comma-operator workaround for regalloc were replaced with direct NULL.
Bot report recorded 6 new matches: ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_1_Anim, ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_0_Anim, ftColl_8007AB48, itseakchain .rodata, grBigBlue_801EF7D8, and fn_801098EC.
Bot report recorded unmatched-item improvements for grbigblue .sdata2 and .rodata.
Review approved the PR but objected to unnecessary extern use for named floats and warned that reverting static variables can create later manual checking work.

smart_moves.lesson: Search specifically for near-100% functions with wrong data values, not just low text similarity.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author noticed recent matches nearly 100% but with wrong data values such as wrong function calls or wrong float literals

smart_moves.lesson: Use a script or targeted query to find functions whose only differences are wrong `bl` instructions; these are efficient near-match fixes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author wrote a script to find all functions that only have wrong `bl` instructions and fixed those

smart_moves.lesson: Diff settings that expose relocation/data-value mismatches are important for near-match cleanup.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author suspected bad matches came from agents not using `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` when diffing

smart_moves.lesson: When a body proves a function's real signature, update its declaration/header rather than keeping a stale local proto.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftAnim_80070458_proto declaration/call replaced by ftAnim_80070458 with u32 tobj_idx and float frame
raw_diff:ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 header signature updated to victim,gobj

smart_moves.lesson: Remove fake-match locals and comments once a direct source form works.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftKb_SpecialNYs.c removed `FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc` workaround and passed NULL directly

decomp_lessons.lesson: A function can look nearly matched while still having semantically wrong relocation values, especially call targets and float arguments. Treat wrong `bl` instructions and wrong float literals/data references as real match failures, not cosmetic diff noise.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:wrong data values include calling the wrong function or passing the wrong float literal
raw_diff:multiple call-target swaps, including it_802AEAB4 -> it_802ADC34 and ftAnim_8006EBA4 -> ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070
raw_diff:multiple ChangeMotionState arguments changed between inline literals and ftKb_Init_* float symbols

decomp_lessons.lesson: For near-match auditing, include data-value relocation checking such as `functionRelocDiffs=data_value`; otherwise wrong calls and constants may be missed by match reporting.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author suspected errors came from not using `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` when diffing
issue_comment:author asks whether match reporting cares about data values because there are more fixes than the bot says

decomp_lessons.lesson: Start from m2c or another relocation-faithful baseline when repairing near-matches; skipping it can leave plausible-looking but wrong call/data values.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author guessed errors were produced by agents that either did not use `m2c` as a starting point or did not use data-value relocation diffing

decomp_lessons.lesson: A signature correction can be part of a matching fix: PR evidence updated both a local declaration/call and a header declaration when argument evidence changed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftAnim_80070458_proto -> ftAnim_80070458(Fighter*, CostumeTObjList*, u32, float)
raw_diff:ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 header changed to victim and gobj parameters

decomp_lessons.lesson: Avoid leaving artificial regalloc helpers when the direct expression matches; fake locals and comma-operator workarounds are review noise and should be removed if unnecessary.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:removed dummy `new_var`, `new_var2`, and fake-match comma-operator comment from fn_801098EC

naming_conventions.convention: Use meaningful argument names when a call relationship is clear: ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78 parameters were named `victim` and `gobj` rather than two anonymous Fighter_GObj pointers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftKb_Init.h signature changed to `Fighter_GObj* victim, Fighter_GObj* gobj`
raw_diff:call site uses `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78(victim, gobj)`

naming_conventions.convention: Do not infer that newly introduced ftKb_Init_804D9xxx float extern names are preferred style; reviewer said these named floats were unnecessary in this case.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya says try not to use extern at all except defining a data symbol in a header; named floats introduced here are not necessary

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Audit near-matches for wrong branch-and-link relocation targets by searching for functions whose only wrong instructions are `bl` instructions.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author wrote a script to find all functions that only have wrong `bl` instructions

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` when diffing near-matches so wrong call targets, float literals, and data references are surfaced.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author explicitly names `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` as likely missing from bad workflows

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Check call order as well as call identity in near-matches; ftKb_EatTurn_Anim changed the order of ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070 and ftAnim_8006EBA4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftKb_EatTurn_Anim moved ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070 before ftAnim_8006EBA4

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Do not preserve dummy locals or comma-operator regalloc workarounds if direct NULL or literal assignment now matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:fn_801098EC dummy locals and fake-match comma operator removed
raw_diff:ftKb_MsSpecialAirNLoop_Anim removed `new_var` and assigned `fp->cmd_vars[0] = 1` directly

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Be cautious with introducing extern float symbols for matching; reviewer preferred leaving duplicate floats to later file-splitting/data ownership work when not necessary.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya says named floats are not necessary and duplicate floats can be deduced by whomever splits the file

review_feedback.feedback: Try not to use `extern` at all except for the specific case of defining a data symbol in a header; this PR introduced named floats that the reviewer considered unnecessary.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:review by ribbanya at 2026-03-15T18:39:53Z

review_feedback.feedback: Even if duplicate floats are used, that can be deduced later by whoever splits the file.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:review body says duplicate floats can be deduced by whomever splits the file

review_feedback.feedback: Static variables currently need to be checked manually, so reverting them when they are actually static can create more work later.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:review body warns about static variables needing manual checks

review_feedback.feedback: The extern/static concerns were not considered worth blocking the merge.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:review body says neither issue was worth blocking a merge

follow_up_queries: Search future near-match PRs for `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` usage or absence when wrong calls/float literals are later corrected.
Find other PRs with review feedback about unnecessary `extern` float symbols or data symbols introduced for code matching.
Search for `FAKE MATCH` comments removed in later PRs to identify patterns where artificial regalloc helpers were unnecessary.
Search for functions corrected only by `bl` target changes to build a corpus of relocation-value near-match failures.
Look for bot reports where matched-code improvements undercount data-value corrections mentioned by authors.
Search for Kirby copied-power collision helpers ft_80082708, ft_800827A0, ft_80081D0C, ftCommon_8007D5D4, and ftCommon_8007D7FC in near-match fixes.

The loaded raw diff is truncated, so exact hunk details are unavailable for later files such as gm_18A5, gmresult, grbigblue route, grvenom, item files, toy, hsd_3AA7, and particle beyond changed-file metadata and bot report summaries.
No inline review comments were present; review feedback comes from one approving review body.
No local build, objdiff, or checkdiff logs were loaded beyond the decomp-dev bot report. Claimed new matches are attributed to the bot report only.
The author's explanation includes a guess about how errors were introduced; this intake preserves it as author speculation rather than accepted causality.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Near-100% matches still need relocation/data-value auditing
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2294 shows that functions can be nearly or fully text-shaped while still using wrong data values, including wrong `bl` call targets and wrong float literals/data references. Near-match cleanup should explicitly check relocation/data values, not only instruction similarity.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:wrong data values like calling wrong function or passing wrong float literal
raw_diff:call-target and float-argument corrections
text_corpus:author asks whether match reporting cares about data values

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use data-value relocation diffing for near-match repair
accepted_candidate_records.text: When repairing near-matches, diff with settings that expose relocation/data-value mismatches such as `functionRelocDiffs=data_value`; PR #2294's author suspected absence of that setting contributed to wrong calls and constants.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author explicitly names `functionRelocDiffs=data_value`

accepted_candidate_records.title: Find wrong-call near-matches by searching for functions with only bad `bl` instructions
accepted_candidate_records.text: A targeted script/query for functions whose only wrong instructions are `bl` instructions can identify efficient near-match fixes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:author wrote a script to find functions with only wrong `bl` instructions

accepted_candidate_records.title: Remove fake regalloc helpers when direct source forms match
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2294 removed dummy locals and an explicit fake-match comma-operator workaround from fn_801098EC and used direct NULL, reinforcing that artificial regalloc scaffolding should not remain when clean source matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:fn_801098EC removed dummy locals and fake-match comma-operator comment

source_update_candidates.title: Explicitly require relocation/data-value checking for near-match claims
source_update_candidates.text: Consider extending verification standards to say that near-match or match claims should check relocation/data values, especially `bl` targets and float/data references, using tooling such as `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` when applicable.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:wrong data values and `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` discussion
text_corpus:author says bot appears to undercount data-value fixes
source_update_candidates.reason: Current loaded standards require verifier evidence generally, but PR #2294 provides concrete evidence that text similarity and bot reports may miss relocation/data-value mismatches.

source_update_candidates.title: Discourage unnecessary extern float/data declarations in code-matching PRs
source_update_candidates.text: Consider a standard or note that code-matching PRs should avoid introducing `extern` float/data symbols solely to name duplicate literals unless data ownership/split evidence requires it; reviewers may prefer leaving duplicate floats for later file-splitting/data ownership work.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya says try not to use extern except defining a data symbol in a header; named floats in this PR are not necessary
review:ribbanya says duplicate floats can be deduced by whomever splits the file
source_update_candidates.reason: Reviewer feedback aligns with existing literal/data ownership standards but may warrant a more explicit note about extern float declarations.

source_update_candidates.title: Warn about reverting static variables during near-match cleanup
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that reverting or de-staticing variables during cleanup should be done cautiously because static variables may require manual checking later.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya warns that static variables currently need manual checking and reverting them can create more work later
source_update_candidates.reason: PR review contains explicit process feedback, but the exact changed static variables are not visible in the loaded diff excerpt, so this should remain a curator-reviewed candidate.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the author's speculation that agents caused the errors by not using m2c or data-value diffing as proven causality; preserve it as a suspected workflow issue.
Do not infer that ftKb_Init_804D9xxx extern float names are accepted style; reviewer explicitly said named floats introduced here were unnecessary.
Do not claim exact fixes for every one of the 24 files; the loaded diff excerpt is truncated and only changed-file metadata is available for several files.
Do not claim this PR validates Claude-generated searching as a standard workflow; PR body says Claude searched for easier fixes, but no review/validation evidence establishes an accepted AI workflow.
Do not promote bot report output as complete validation of all fixes; the author specifically questioned whether the report cared about data values.
